value.found_quote: In TypeScript, the fastest path to a browser-based voice assistant is a `RealtimeAgent` and `RealtimeSession`... From there, attach tools, handoffs, and guardrails to the `RealtimeAgent` the same way you would attach them to a text agent. Keep audio transport concerns in the session layer, and keep business logic in the agent definition.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes `RealtimeAgent` as a dedicated agent type for speech-to-speech (live audio) sessions in the TypeScript SDK. It shows a code example importing `RealtimeAgent` from `@openai/agents/realtime`, and directly states that tools, handoffs, and guardrails can be attached to the `RealtimeAgent` — matching all aspects of the claim. The page presents this as a current, active offering with no indication of preview or deprecation status.
